etymology.description: semantic 力(力) (lì, "strength") + phonetic 巠(巠) (jīng, "stream")
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 力(力) (lì, "strength") directly conveys the meaning of physical power or strength. The phonetic component 巠(巠) (jīng, "stream") contributes the pronunciation, connecting the imagery of flowing energy, which aligns with the sense of strength or vitality. This combination gives the character 勁(劲) (jìn, "strength; energy; enthusiasm"), carrying the sense of both physical and enthusiastic vigor.
etymology.simplification: 劲 is the simplified form of 勁, where the character 巠(巠) (jīng, "stream") was removed, simplifying the stroke count while retaining 力(力) (lì, "strength") to maintain the core meaning.

examples.chinese: 他踢足球的时候非常有劲(他踢足球的時候非常有勁)。
examples.pinyin: Tā tī zúqiú de shíhòu fēicháng yǒu jìn.
examples.english: He is very energetic when playing football.

examples.chinese: 这道菜有一种特别的劲(這道菜有一種特別的勁)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dào cài yǒu yī zhǒng tèbié de jìn.
examples.english: This dish has a special kick.

examples.chinese: 加油！我们还有劲(加油！我們還有勁)。
examples.english: Keep it up! We still have energy.
